failure_surface: ValueError: Invalid column index C during verification loop - the agent passed column letter strings to get_column_letter() which expects integer column indices
root_cause: Misunderstanding of openpyxl.column_label_to_number vs get_column_letter API direction. The agent called openpyxl.utils.get_column_letter('C') when it should have used openpyxl.utils.column_index_from_string('C') or simply used the numeric column index directly.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before calling spreadsheet library utility functions for coordinate conversion, verify the function signature - get_column_letter() converts NUMBER→LETTER while column_index_from_string() converts LETTER→NUMBER
evidence: Line 65 shows: cell_val = ws_verify.cell(row=row_num, column=openpyxl.utils.get_column_letter(col_letter)).value where col_letter='C'
Error traceback: ValueError: Invalid column index C - indicates get_column_letter received a string instead of integer
Agent already defined col_map with correct numeric mappings earlier but didn't use them in verification loop
Verification loop iterates over column letters ['C', 'D', 'E', 'F', 'G'] instead of numeric indices [3, 4, 5, 6, 7]

memory_items.title: openpyxl Column Conversion Direction
memory_items.lesson: When converting between Excel column notation and indices, use column_index_from_string() for letter-to-number and get_column_letter() for number-to-letter
memory_items.generalization: Always verify the directionality of spreadsheet library coordinate conversion utilities before calling them; mixing up input types causes immediate crashes

memory_items.title: Consistent Index Usage Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Use numeric column/row indices throughout processing loops for consistency; convert to display format only when needed for logging or user output
memory_items.generalization: Standardizing on one coordinate representation reduces type-mismatch errors in iteration and cell access patterns

memory_items.title: Library Function Signature Verification
memory_items.lesson: When writing verification or helper code that uses external library functions, include type validation or sanity checks before invoking with dynamic values
memory_items.generalization: Type mismatches in library calls often occur in secondary/verification code paths rather than primary logic

edits.content: - **Column Utilities**: When converting between Excel column letters and numbers, remember openpyxl.utils.get_column_letter() takes an integer and returns a letter, while column_index_from_string() takes a letter and returns an integer. Verify function directionality before calling.
